overview.text: Qwen is Alibaba Cloud's large-language-model family, launched in beta as Tongyi Qianwen in April 2023 and opened for public use that September, developed out of Alibaba Cloud's Hangzhou headquarters and released under a mix of open and proprietary licenses.

citations.quote: Alibaba launched a beta of Qwen in April 2023

what_theyve_done.text: Alibaba released Qwen 7B's weights in August 2023, then a set of 72B and 1.8B models that December, followed by Qwen2 in June 2024 and the Apache-2.0-licensed Qwen3 family in April 2025.

citations.quote: all models licensed under the Apache 2.0 license

what_theyve_done.text: It released Qwen3.5 and Qwen3.5-Plus in February 2026, and then Qwen3.7-Max on May 21, 2026 as its coding- and office-agent flagship, sustaining a 35-hour autonomous kernel-optimization run with over 1,000 tool calls.

citations.quote: a 35-hour, fully autonomous kernel optimization run

strengths.text: Qwen3.7-Max generalizes across different agent frameworks -- Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Alibaba's own Qwen Code -- rather than being tuned to perform well only inside one harness.
Alibaba reports Qwen3.7-Max leading its own comparison on GPQA Diamond, ahead of Anthropic's Opus-4.6, among other advanced reasoning benchmarks.

current_focus.text: Alibaba's current focus for Qwen is long-horizon agentic execution -- sustaining autonomous coding and office-automation sessions across hundreds or thousands of steps -- served as a closed API through Alibaba Cloud Model Studio rather than as downloadable weights for its top-tier Max model.

citations.quote: sustaining autonomous execution across hundreds or thousands of steps
